title: danthonia × breviaristata
intro: Danthonia × breviaristata, also known as Danthonia grass or wild oat grass, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the grass family, Poaceae. It is native to North America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Herbal preparations of Danthonia × breviaristata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat a variety of health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
